notes:
I think the game is too fresh today for linux platform.

Directly from support

"At this time, Subnautica 2 officially supports only 64-bit versions of Windows 10 and Windows 11. Since the game is currently in Early Access, the development team is focusing on delivering and optimizing the best possible experience for Windows PC and Xbox players first.

While there is no confirmed support for macOS or Linux right now, the team is actively working on compatibility for handheld gaming devices such as Steam Deck and the ROG Xbox Ally series. We know platform availability is important to our community, and any future updates regarding additional platform support will be shared through our official channels."

gpu: AMD Radeon RX 6700 (radeonsi, navi22, ACO, DRM 3.64, 7.0.5-1-cachyos)
title: Subnautica 2

notes:
Only getting to the black loading screen before crashing after 5 minutes. It seams like a GPU problem.

A small resume:
  Status: Crashes on first render frame, never reaches menu.

  Crash sequence (from logs):
  1. Game loads fully --- assets, Steam auth, UI all initialize correctly
  2. First 3D frame begins rendering
  3. LogRenderer: Forcing update for all mesh draw commands: SkyLight real-time capture change
  4. Both 3D and Compute GPU queues time out simultaneously after exactly 5 seconds
  5. DXGI_ERROR_DEVICE_REMOVED --- D3D device lost, 0MB VRAM reported

  Root cause: VKD3D-Proton fails to maintain the D3D12 device through the first rendered frame on Blackwell. The memory budget extension (VK_EXT_memory_budget) reports 0MB for both local and system budget at
  crash time, suggesting the Vulkan device is lost during initial rendering. The 5-second exact timeout on both queues simultaneously points to a cross-queue synchronization issue in VKD3D-Proton with GB203.

  Workarounds attempted (none worked):
  - PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1
  - VKD3D_CONFIG=no_upload_hvv, nodxr, single_queue
  - VKD3D_DISABLE_EXTENSIONS=VK_EXT_memory_budget
  - Disabling Streamline interposer (sl.interposer.dll)
  - All scalability settings at 0, various resolutions
  - r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture=0 via Engine.ini
title: Subnautica 2
